free_text: Differential cross sections of 12C(d,p1)13C and
10B(d,p)11B below E(d)=3.0 MeV
Semiconductor detectors (300 mu-m)
Error in the calibration detector angle.
CNPD: corrected 4-dig. year, lower case.
SD: Corrections in all Subents.
SAMPLE moved to Subents 002-005.
Thin self-supporting 12C targets were prepared from a
suspension of graphite in alcohol. The thicknesses of
these targets were about 40 ug/cm2.
No information about source of uncertainty
(from graphic bars, probably statistical).
Because of resonances in this energy range,
it is estimated that the absolute cross section
scales for the curves at Ed = 0.900, 1.320, 1.370,
1.490, 1.690 and 1.760 MeV may be in error by 30%,
and that at 1.452 MeV the scale may be in error by
50%. The remaining absolute sections are estimated to
be accurate to 20%.
figs 2,3 from Nucl.Phys.,A92(1967)97
SD+SS: SF9=EXP deleted from REACTION code.
BIB updated. Accuracy of digitized data presentation
revised. ANG2-ERR -> ANG-ERR-D; DATA-ERR -> ERR-DIG;
DATA-ERR1 -> DATA-ERR. Data at E=0.9 MeV were
re-digitized.
Thin self-supporting 10B targets were made by electron
gun evaporation. The amount of 10B present was about
130 ug/cm2.
No information about source of uncertainty.
Total r.m.s. errors including the absolute
normalization
